name: 4000 Essential English Words (Nation)
description: Vocab graduado Paul Nation/Compass. Cada card: palavra + classe gramatical + definição em inglês + exemplo. Mistura A1-C1 (decks 1-6) — use SRS pra calibrar pelo seu nível.

back.text: To agree is to have the same opinion or belief as another person.

examples.en: The students agree they have too much homework.

back.text: Alcohol is a type of drink that can make people drunk.

examples.en: A person should not drive a car after he or she has been drinking alcohol.

back.text: To arrive is to get somewhere.

examples.en: They arrived at school at 7 a.m.

back.text: August is the eighth month of the year.

examples.en: Is your birthday in August?

back.text: A boat is a vehicle that moves across water.

examples.en: There is a small boat on the lake.

back.text: Breakfast is the morning meal.

examples.en: I ate eggs for breakfast.

back.text: A camera is a piece of equipment that takes pictures.

examples.en: I brought my camera on my vacation.

back.text: A capital is a city where a country’s government is based.

examples.en: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C.

back.text: To catch is to grab or get something.

examples.en: Did you catch the ball during the baseball game?

back.text: A duck is a small water bird.

examples.en: People feed ducks at the lake.

back.text: To enjoy is to like something.

examples.en: The woman enjoys riding her bicycle.

back.text: To invite is to ask someone to come to a place or event.

examples.en: I will invite my friends to my birthday party.

back.text: To love is to like something or someone a lot.

examples.en: I love my family very much.

back.text: A month is one of 12 periods of time in one year.

examples.en: January is the first month of the year.

back.text: To travel is to go to a faraway place on vacation or business.

examples.en: They will travel to Argentina this summer.

back.text: If something is typical, it is normal, or something that usually happens.

examples.en: My typical breakfast is toast and eggs.

back.text: To visit is to go and spend time in another place or see another person.

examples.en: She wants to visit her grandmother.

back.text: Weather is the temperature and the state of the outdoors.

examples.en: Today’s weather is rainy and cloudy.

back.text: A week is a period of time that is seven days long.

examples.en: What are you doing next week?

back.text: Wine is an alcoholic drink made from grapes.

examples.en: The store carried both red and white wine.

back.text: An adventure is a fun or exciting thing that you do.

examples.en: Riding in the rough water was an adventure.

back.text: To approach something means to move close to it.

examples.en: The boy approached his school.

back.text: Carefully means with great attention, especially to detail or safety.

examples.en: The baby carefully climbed down the stairs.

back.text: A chemical is something that scientists use in chemistry.

examples.en: The scientist mixed the chemicals.

back.text: To create means to make something new.

examples.en: She created an igloo from blocks of snow.

back.text: Evil describes something or someone bad or cruel, not good.

examples.en: They felt a strange, evil presence as they got closer to the house.

back.text: An experiment is a test that you do to see what will happen.

examples.en: The student did an experiment in science class.

back.text: To kill someone or something is to make them die.

examples.en: I killed the fly.

back.text: A laboratory is a room where a scientist works.

examples.en: My mother works in a laboratory.

back.text: Laugh is the sound made when someone is happy or a funny thing occurs.

examples.en: The sound of her laugh filled the room.

back.text: If a sound is loud, it is strong and very easy to hear.

examples.en: The man’s voice was so loud that we all could hear him.

back.text: When a person is nervous, they think something bad will happen.

examples.en: The boy became nervous when he heard the news.

back.text: A noise is an unpleasant sound.

examples.en: The crying baby made a loud noise.

back.text: A project is a type of work that you do for school or a job.

examples.en: His afternoon work project was to paint the room green.

back.text: To scare someone is to make them feel afraid.

examples.en: My uncle was scared by what he saw in the room.

back.text: A secret is something that you do not tell other people.

examples.en: The two boys were sharing a secret.

back.text: To shout is to say something loudly.

examples.en: My boss shouted at me because I was late for work.

back.text: To smell something means to use your nose to sense it.

examples.en: The two friends smelled the flower.

back.text: If something is terrible, it is very bad.

examples.en: The way he treated his classmate was terrible.

back.text: If something is worse, it is of poorer quality than another thing.

examples.en: Business was worse this month than last month.

back.text: Because introduces a reason for something.

examples.en: We need to study because we have a test tomorrow.

back.text: East is the direction the sun rises from.

examples.en: My window looks to the east, so I can watch the sunrise every morning.

back.text: Expensive things cost a lot of money.

examples.en: My friend drives an expensive sports car.

back.text: A flower is the colored part of a plant.

examples.en: She gave pink flowers to her grandmother.

back.text: A garden is an area where people grow plants.

examples.en: The garden is very bright and colorful in the spring.

back.text: A holiday is a special day of celebration.

examples.en: Monday was a holiday, so there was no school or work.

back.text: Many shows that there is a large number of something.

examples.en: There are many people on the street.

back.text: A million is another way to write the number 1,000,000.

examples.en: Almost 19 million people live in Delhi, India.

back.text: A mountain is a very high hill.

examples.en: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.

back.text: A place is a space or area.

examples.en: A library is a place where people can read books.

back.text: A popular thing is liked by many people.

examples.en: These people are listening to a popular man speak.

back.text: To ski is to glide on long pieces of wood or metal over snow.

examples.en: The man likes to ski and goes every weekend.

back.text: Such means “like this”.

examples.en: I have never seen such a beautiful sunset before.

back.text: Total shows that everyone or everything has been counted

examples.en: .The total cost of the items she bought was $52.

back.text: A tower is a tall, narrow building.

examples.en: This is a very famous tower in Italy.

back.text: A town is a place where people live and work, and is smaller than a city.

examples.en: I come from a small town, and everyone there knows each other.

back.text: A train is a group of railway cars connected together.

examples.en: The train is very fast, so we can get home in one hour.

back.text: To walk is to move forward using legs, but it is slower than running.

examples.en: The children walk to school in the morning.

back.text: To watch is to look at someone or something for a period of time.

examples.en: My friend came over to watch a movie with me.

back.text: The world is the Earth and all the people and things in it.

examples.en: What are the names of the five oceans of the world?

back.text: Anxious means feeling worried or nervous.

examples.en: She was anxious about not making her appointment on time.

back.text: An awful thing is very bad.

examples.en: Her performance last night was awful.

back.text: To consist of certain is to be made of parts or things them.

examples.en: Today’s choices for lunch consisted of pizza, hamburgers, and hot dogs.

back.text: To desire is to want something.

examples.en: My sister desires a big house and lots of money.

back.text: Eager shows excitement about something.

examples.en: The man was eager to talk about the good news.

back.text: A household is all the people who live in one house.

examples.en: Our household is made up of my father, my mother, and me.

back.text: An intent is a plan to do something.

examples.en: Her intent is to visit Italy next summer.

back.text: A landscape is how an area of land looks.

examples.en: The landscape of the country is very green.

back.text: To lift something is to move it higher.

examples.en: The man tried to lift the box.

back.text: To load is to put objects into something.

examples.en: The man loaded the boxes into a truck.

back.text: A lung is a part of the body that fills with air when breathing.

examples.en: Having strong lungs is necessary for a healthy life.

back.text: A motion is a movement that someone makes.

examples.en: The police officer made a motion with his hand.

back.text: The pace of something is the speed at which it happens.

examples.en: I ran the race at a slower pace than my friend.

back.text: Polite shows a thoughtful and kind behavior.

examples.en: The boy was very polite; he behaved very thoughtfully.

back.text: To possess something is to have it or own it.

examples.en: My uncle possesses three sheep, a chicken, a cow, and a dog.

back.text: Rapidly means happening very fast.

examples.en: The train moved rapidly on the tracks.

back.text: To remark is to say something.

examples.en: The teacher remarked on how quickly the students were learning.

back.text: To seek is to look for something.

examples.en: If I have a problem, I seek my sister’s advice.

back.text: To shine is to make a bright light.

examples.en: The candles are shining in the dark room.

back.text: To spill is to accidentally make something fall out of its container.

examples.en: I spilled the coffee on the table.

back.text: An acre is a unit for measuring area.

examples.en: They lived on a 150-acre farm.

back.text: The afterlife is a life that some people believe begins when a person dies.

examples.en: I believe that there is an afterlife.

back.text: Archaeology is the study of the remains left by ancient societies.

examples.en: He enjoyed visiting the Great Pyramids of Egypt because he loves archaeology.

back.text: A chamber is a closed space or room used for a special purpose.

examples.en: The meeting was held in the faculty chamber.

back.text: A channel is a long, deep space between two edges.

examples.en: The river cut a channel through the rocks.

back.text: A core is the main or central part of something.

examples.en: Earth has a solid inner core.

back.text: A corridor is a narrow passage that leads into other areas.

examples.en: He took the corridor on the left to go to his office.

back.text: A distinct thing is different or stands out.

examples.en: He has a distinct accent.

back.text: Elite means of or from a high-level group.

examples.en: Only an elite group was allowed membership into the club.

back.text: To engineer something is to skillfully plan out how to make that thing.

examples.en: After engineering the robot, they needed to find the correct parts to build it.

back.text: To found something means to start, organize, or establish that thing.

examples.en: My grandfather founded the City Bank.

back.text: A gap is a space between two things.

examples.en: There is a small gap between the blocks of wood.

back.text: Glory is the importance, magnificence, or specialness of something.

examples.en: They enjoyed the glory of the beautiful sunset.

back.text: An interior is the inside of something.

examples.en: They looked at the interior of the box.

back.text: A lion is a large animal in the cat family.

examples.en: We went to see the lion at the zoo.

back.text: A role is a job, position, or part in something.

examples.en: Her role in the office is to sell products to customers.

back.text: Royal means related to a king or queen.

examples.en: The Duchess was part of the royal family.

back.text: A sole person or thing is the only one.

examples.en: She was the sole woman in the room.

back.text: Stairs are a set of steps built to go from one level of a building to another.

examples.en: He took the stairs instead of the elevator.

back.text: The surface is the top layer of something.

examples.en: She wiped the surface of the table.

back.text: An agreement is a formal decision about future action.

examples.en: I think you’ll get Tom’s agreement to this proposal.

back.text: To arise is to happen.

examples.en: Difficulties arose with his computer because it was old.

back.text: A benefactor is a person who gives money to help someone.

examples.en: The student’s benefactor gave him money to spend on his studies.

back.text: A blacksmith is a person who makes things out of metal.

examples.en: The blacksmith pounded the piece of metal until it was flat.

back.text: A chimney is a tall pipe used to carry smoke out of a building.

examples.en: The cat was sitting on the roof next to the chimney.

back.text: To compensate is to pay someone for the time they spent doing something.

examples.en: Her boss compensated her for the extra work she did last week.

back.text: To encounter is to find or meet a person or thing.

examples.en: I encountered a sea turtle while I was swimming.

back.text: To exceed is to be more than something.

examples.en: Since I exceeded my limit, I decided to get rid of my credit cards.

back.text: To forge is to make or produce, especially with difficulty.

examples.en: Stacy and Heather forged their friendship when they were teenagers.

back.text: People who are humble do not believe that they are better than other people.

examples.en: Even though Bob is the smartest boy in his class, he is humble.

back.text: Iron is a strong metal that is used to make many objects.

examples.en: The horse had shoes made of iron.

back.text: A ladder is an object that is used to climb up and down things.

examples.en: He used a ladder to climb to the top of his tree house.

back.text: If people are modest, they do not think that they are too important.

examples.en: Derek is very modest for someone who is so rich.

back.text: To occupy a place is to live, work, or be there.

examples.en: Kevin and Alice occupied the chairs and had a long discussion.

back.text: A penny is a coin worth one cent.

examples.en: US President Abraham Lincoln is on the penny.

back.text: To preach is to talk about and promote a religious idea.

examples.en: Aaron often preached about living an honest life.

back.text: To prosper is to be successful or make a lot of money.

examples.en: Frank’s new business finally prospered after many years of hard work.

back.text: A province is an area that is controlled by a country.

examples.en: Canada is divided into several different provinces.

back.text: Satisfaction is the feeling of having done or received something good.

examples.en: Brad was filled with satisfaction when he saw what was for dinner.

back.text: To sustain something is to keep it going.

examples.en: Wind power is a clean way to sustain a city with energy.

back.text: When someone is accountable, he or she is responsible for the effects of his or her actions.

examples.en: Political leaders are accountable to the people they represent.

back.text: If something is addictive, it is hard to stop doing.

examples.en: I think this game is very addictive.

back.text: An agenda is a plan or goal that guides someone’s behavior.

examples.en: The man is pushing his questionable agenda on his colleagues.

back.text: To coin is to create a new word or phrase that other people begin to use.

examples.en: The coach coined the phrase, “There is no ‘I’ in team.”

back.text: To correlate is to have a close connection to something.

examples.en: Scientists believe that brain size might correlate with intelligence.

back.text: Depression is a medical condition that makes a person very unhappy for long periods of time.

examples.en: Ron decided to get help for his depression.

back.text: When something is digital, it is characterized by computer technology.

examples.en: Students prefer to take notes on their laptops and other digital devices.

back.text: Importance is the quality or state of being important, valuable, or significant.

examples.en: The professor lectured the students on the importance of communication strategies.

back.text: A keyboard has buttons marked with letters and numbers that are pressed to put information into a computer.

examples.en: Paul spilled coffee all over his keyboard.

back.text: Loneliness is the unhappiness that is felt by someone if they do not have any friends.

examples.en: Many people join social clubs to escape loneliness.

back.text: Media is the different ways of entertaining and giving information to the public.

examples.en: Many people like to express their opinions online using social media.

back.text: A platform is something that allows someone to tell a large number of people about an idea, product, etc.

examples.en: The band uses their music as a platform to express their political beliefs.

back.text: When something is poor, it is not as good as it could or should be.

examples.en: The condition of the sidewalk is very poor.

back.text: Presence is when someone or something is present in a particular place.

examples.en: The researcher discovered the presence of bacteria in the water sample.

back.text: To subject is to cause or force someone or something to experience something harmful or unpleasant.

examples.en: The location of the trees will subject them to strong winds from the hurricane.

back.text: A surge is a sudden, large increase in something.

examples.en: All at once, there was a surge of runners at the finish line.

back.text: A survey is a set of questions that are asked to a large number of people in order to find out about opinions.

examples.en: I filled out a survey about my shopping experience at the department store.

back.text: To target is to aim an attack at someone or something.

examples.en: Scientists are looking for new drugs to target cancer cells.

back.text: A trend is a general tendency in the way a situation is changing or developing.

examples.en: Lucy likes to think she started this fashion trend.

back.text: To validate is to make someone feel that his or her ideas and feelings are respected.

examples.en: Winning first prize will validate all of Tony’s hard work.

back.text: Carbon is a chemical element.

examples.en: Carbon is found in coal.

back.text: Carbon dioxide is a gas made from one carbon atom and two oxygen atoms.

examples.en: There is carbon dioxide in the smoke from coal power plants.

back.text: Dust is very small, dry particles of earth or sand.

examples.en: Some people wear medical face masks to avoid breathing in fine dust and germs.

back.text: To emit is to send out gas, heat, light, sound, etc.

examples.en: This iron emits steam.

back.text: To enable is to make it possible for something to happen.

examples.en: Studying hard will enable the girl to get a good job.

back.text: To grind is to break something into small pieces or powder.

examples.en: We grind wheat to make flour.

back.text: To heal is to become healthy or well again.

examples.en: This ointment will help heal your wounds.

back.text: To integrate is to make something a part of another larger thing.

examples.en: More women are being integrated into the military.

back.text: When something is leading, it is the most advanced or best.

examples.en: Teresa is the leading expert on brain surgery for dogs.

back.text: Moreover is used to introduce information that adds to or supports what has previously been said.

examples.en: Marianne likes to walk to work to save money; moreover, it is good exercise.

back.text: To object is to feel or say that you oppose or disapprove of something.

examples.en: The students object to raising the tuition.

back.text: A pollutant is a substance that makes the air or water not safe for use.

examples.en: A pollutant in the water killed this fish.

back.text: Powder is a dry substance in the form of very small grains.

examples.en: Turmeric is a yellow powder used in cooking.

back.text: To prompt is to cause someone to do something.

examples.en: The tight deadline will prompt the employee to work very hard.

back.text: To reject is to refuse to accept, believe in, or agree with something.

examples.en: I’m afraid the teacher will reject my new idea.

back.text: A skeptic is a person who disagrees with particular claims that are generally thought to be true.

examples.en: Kevin is a skeptic because he never believes anyone or anything.

back.text: A substance is a particular type of solid, liquid, or gas.

examples.en: Arsenic is a poisonous substance.

back.text: If something changes substantially, it changes a lot.

examples.en: The price of the apartment went up substantially.

back.text: A ton is a unit for measuring weight equal to 2000 pounds or 907.2 kilograms.

examples.en: This dump truck can carry up to 25 tons.

back.text: Transport is the system or method of carrying passengers or goods from one place to another.

examples.en: Cargo ships are used for the transport of manufactured goods.

back.text: An aroma is a scent or smell.

examples.en: I love the aroma of coffee in the morning.

back.text: A beverage is a drink.

examples.en: The waiter brought our beverages first. Then he brought our food.

back.text: A cluster of things is a small group of them placed close together.

examples.en: She held a large cluster of grapes in her hand.

back.text: To combine is to join together to make a single thing or group.

examples.en: Mina combined peanut butter and jelly to make a sandwich.

back.text: When a liquid is condensed, it is made thicker.

examples.en: One way to make a dessert thick and sweet is to use condensed milk.

back.text: When something is contemporary, it is related to the present time.

examples.en: Contemporary scientists have learned quite a bit about DNA.

back.text: To cultivate plants is to care for them and help them grow.

examples.en: A research company is cultivating new kinds of rice to aid poor countries.

back.text: When something is divine, it is related to gods.

examples.en: Legends say that music was given to humans as a divine gift from the gods.

back.text: When it is humid, there is a lot of water in the air.

examples.en: It is very humid inside a sauna.

back.text: An odor is a very distinct smell.

examples.en: He knew there was a leak when he noticed the strong odor of natural gas.

back.text: The palate is the top part of the mouth.

examples.en: You can touch your palate with your tongue.

back.text: Paradise is the place or condition of happiness where things are perfect.

examples.en: My vacation in Hawaii was like being in paradise.

back.text: A plantation is a big farm that only grows certain kinds of crops.

examples.en: In the 1800s, there were many cotton plantations in the southern US.

back.text: When something is rapid, it moves or changes very quickly.

examples.en: His mother was surprised by her son’s rapid growth.

back.text: A rate is the speed at which something happens.

examples.en: Grass tends to grow at a very slow rate.

back.text: When something is soothing, it makes you calm or relaxed.

examples.en: The soothing music helped the baby fall asleep.

back.text: When something is subtle, it is not easy to see or notice.

examples.en: The handsome man has a subtle smile.

back.text: The texture of something is the way its surface looks and feels.

examples.en: The texture of a rock found in the water is typically very smooth.

back.text: When something is toxic, it is poisonous and very dangerous.

examples.en: Please check the label to see if the product is toxic.

back.text: To vary means to be different from another thing in size or amount.

examples.en: The heights of the people in my class vary by a large amount.

back.text: An admiral is someone who controls many military ships.

examples.en: They won the sea battle because of the admiral’s great leadership.

back.text: An arc is a curved shape.

examples.en: A rainbow is an arc of colored bands across the sky.

back.text: Your character is your personality.

examples.en: My sister’s character is fun and very outgoing.

back.text: Your conscience is your inner sense of what is right and wrong.

examples.en: I recycle everything I can, so my conscience is clear!

back.text: If you escape from a place, you succeed in getting away from it.

examples.en: She tried to escape from the building.

back.text: If something is fiery, it is burning strongly.

examples.en: The fiery blaze burned all night long.

back.text: Flesh is the muscle and fat on your body.

examples.en: The zebra’s flesh is covered by black and white skin.

back.text: A grapefruit is a fruit similar to an orange, but bigger and not as sweet.

examples.en: Would you like a grapefruit with your breakfast?

back.text: Hay is dry grass used to feed animals and can be used for different purposes.

examples.en: I need to buy some more hay for the horse to eat.

back.text: If you are horrified, you are very shocked and feel upset.

examples.en: I was horrified when I read about the old lady who was attacked.

back.text: Kerosene is a type of oil used in some lamps and stoves.

examples.en: Many people in poor countries cook on kerosene stoves.

back.text: A loop is a line made into the shape of a circle.

examples.en: He made a loop with the rope and placed it over the post.

back.text: The middle is nearest the center and with an equal number of things on each side.

examples.en: I suddenly woke up in the middle of the night.

back.text: A paddle is a piece of wood or plastic that moves a boat across water.

examples.en: Put your paddle in the water and pull hard to make the boat move.

back.text: When something is sour, it has a sharp and unpleasant taste.

examples.en: I don’t like lemons because I think they are too sour.

back.text: A stake is a small, sharp piece of wood or metal that is put into the ground.

examples.en: We marked our property by placing stakes into the ground.

back.text: A steward is a person like a waiter who serves food on planes and ships.

examples.en: The steward is bringing some tea.

back.text: String is a thin piece of fabric or rope.

examples.en: I found a large ball of string.

back.text: A thorn is a sharp part of a plant.

examples.en: Be careful of the thorns when you pick the roses!

back.text: To wreck something means to destroy or ruin it.

examples.en: The teenagers wrecked the house for no reason at all.

back.text: Something that has an adverse effect can be harmful, dangerous, or unfavorable.

examples.en: I worry that the tornado will have an adverse effect on the farm.

back.text: An alternate option is a different option.

examples.en: Taking the bus and driving to work are alternate ways to travel.

back.text: Materials that are biodegradable break down naturally into substances that do not harm the environment.

examples.en: I use biodegradable compost to feed my garden.

back.text: When something is boxed, it is inside a package.

examples.en: Justine brought a boxed gift to the birthday party.

back.text: If you choke on something, it stops you from breathing.

examples.en: The gum Malinda swallowed made her choke.

back.text: When something is convenient, it saves you time or effort.

examples.en: Walking through the park is a convenient way to exercise on the way to work.

back.text: To discard something is to throw it away.

examples.en: After repairing the window, discard any broken glass.

back.text: A dolphin is a large sea mammal that breathes air.

examples.en: Intelligent dolphins have learned to communicate with humans.

back.text: Ecologically means that something is done in a way that concerns living organisms and the environment.

examples.en: We planted our garden according to ecologically correct practices.

back.text: Something that is fatal results in someone’s death.

examples.en: Being shot by the attacker proved fatal to the victim.

back.text: The number of times something happens is the incidence of the event.

examples.en: We need to increase the incidence of success in school.

back.text: Municipal means that something belongs to a city or local government.

examples.en: The municipal parking lot downtown can fit one hundred cars.

back.text: Overuse occurs when something is utilized too many times.

examples.en: Rochelle’s overuse of the exercise bike caused the gears to break.

back.text: A pond is a freshwater body that is smaller than a lake.

examples.en: I learned to swim at the pond behind the recreation center.

back.text: When you recycle an object, you use its parts to make something else.

examples.en: We recycle our newspapers so that they can be made into packing boxes.

back.text: An object that is reusable can be utilized over and over again.

examples.en: Saburo keeps his empty jelly jars because they are reusable for storing sewing supplies.

back.text: When something is stuffed, it is pushed into a small space.

examples.en: The newspapers were all stuffed into the drawer.

back.text: A tragic event causes sadness because it might involve death or suffering.

examples.en: The entire community attended the memorial service for the victims of the tragic fire.

back.text: To utilize something is to use it for a specific purpose.

examples.en: The team can utilize the lab equipment to complete the experiment.

back.text: A whale is a very large mammal that lives in the ocean.

examples.en: We were surprised when a whale surfaced next to our boat.

back.text: An amateur is a person who does something for fun and isn’t paid for it.

examples.en: The amateur took pictures just as well as the person who worked for money.

back.text: If something is ambiguous, it is not entirely clear.

examples.en: It’s ambiguous as to whether a newborn baby looks like a girl or a boy.

back.text: If someone is anonymous, no one knows who he or she is.

examples.en: An anonymous donor gave a thousand dollars to the museum.

back.text: To attain something is to succeed at something or to get something you want.

examples.en: If you want to attain a healthy body, you must exercise every day.

back.text: Autonomy is another word for freedom or independence.

examples.en: In the 1800s, the people of India fought for autonomy from Britain.

back.text: A concession is something that one person gives up to another.

examples.en: China gave Hong Kong to Britain as a concession after the war.

back.text: Decay is the result of something slowly being broken down or destroyed naturally.

examples.en: The decay in the old building was obvious.

back.text: To dwell somewhere means to live there.

examples.en: Before he was a successful writer, Mark Twain dwelled in the city of Hannibal, Missouri.

back.text: To enlighten someone is to teach them about something.

examples.en: Greek philosophers wanted to enlighten the people of Athens with their ideas.

back.text: To enrich means to make someone rich or increase their wealth.

examples.en: Taxes on people’s income can be used to enrich the government.

back.text: To flourish means to do very well and be in an excellent condition.

examples.en: The Roman Empire flourished in Europe two thousand years ago.

back.text: Geometry is the study of shapes and how to measure them.

examples.en: If you want to be able to calculate the area of a circle, you must study geometry.

back.text: To gleam means to sparkle and shine.

examples.en: The waves of the ocean would gleam every night at sunset.

back.text: Greed is wanting to have more of something than you need or should have.

examples.en: The story of King Midas is a story of greed.

back.text: Harmony is a feeling that everything is peaceful, balanced, and in agreement.

examples.en: The United Nations is struggling to bring peace and harmony to the world.

back.text: Indigenous means that something or someone exists naturally in an environment or area.

examples.en: The Pueblo people were an indigenous tribe in Arizona who lived in adobe homes.

back.text: Jurisdiction means the power or right to make judgments about the law and how it is upheld.

examples.en: We did not have jurisdiction to bring the criminal to trial.

back.text: A parade is a series of things or people that come or are shown one after another.

examples.en: This parade of elephants is part of an open-air exhibit to honor these majestic animals.

back.text: A statue is a three-dimensional work of art, usually made of clay, marble, or metal.

examples.en: The Venus de Milo is a famous ancient Greek statue.

back.text: A virgin is someone who has never had sex.

examples.en: The virgin goddess of the moon was known as Diana.
